description: ExamBridge v3.2 unified cross-stage mathematics knowledge tree. Expanded for Further Pure, Further Mechanics, Further Statistics and IAL coverage.

principles: Same knowledge point across IGCSE, A-Level and Further lives in the same sub-tree
Stage coverage is indicated by igcseLevel and alevelLevel fields, not by separate roots
Node IDs are domain-prefixed and do not contain stage prefixes
Mechanics content is only included at GCSE/IGCSE where explicitly covered (e.g. 0606, 4PM1 vectors)
Further Pure, Decision/Discrete and Further Statistics topics are grouped under FURTHER domain

nodes.name: Mathematics
nodes.description: Unified cross-stage mathematics knowledge root

nodes.name: Number
nodes.description: Number concepts, operations, accuracy, fractions, percentages, ratio, proportion and standard form

nodes.name: Algebra and Functions
nodes.description: Algebraic manipulation, equations, inequalities, functions, graphs, sequences and series

nodes.name: Coordinate Geometry
nodes.description: Cartesian coordinates, equations of straight lines, circles, curves and parametric equations

nodes.name: Calculus
nodes.description: Differentiation, integration, differential equations and their applications

nodes.name: Trigonometry
nodes.description: Trigonometric ratios, functions, identities, equations and applications

nodes.name: Geometry and Mensuration
nodes.description: Geometric properties, constructions, similarity, congruence, circle theorems, perimeter, area and volume

nodes.name: Vectors
nodes.description: Vector algebra, position vectors, scalar product, vector equations and 3D vectors

nodes.name: Probability
nodes.description: Probability concepts, combined events, conditional probability and distributions

nodes.name: Statistics
nodes.description: Data collection, presentation, analysis, distributions, hypothesis testing and correlation

nodes.name: Mechanics
nodes.description: Kinematics, forces, Newton's laws, energy, momentum, circular motion and statics

nodes.name: Discrete / Decision / Further
nodes.description: Discrete mathematics, decision algorithms, further pure topics, groups and numerical methods

nodes.name: Operations and Place Value
nodes.description: Integer, decimal and fraction operations; place value; priority of operations

nodes.description: Work with ratios, direct and inverse proportion, scale factors and compound measures

nodes.description: Calculate work done by a variable force using integration and apply it to conservation of energy problems.

nodes.description: Conduct a one-sample t-test for a population mean when the population variance is unknown.

nodes.description: Conduct two-sample and paired t-tests for the difference between two population means.

nodes.description: Formulate hypotheses and carry out tests for the mean of a Poisson distribution.

nodes.name: Hypothesis Tests for Geometric
nodes.description: Conduct hypothesis tests for the parameter of a geometric distribution.

nodes.description: Formulate hypotheses and carry out tests for the probability parameter of a geometric distribution.

nodes.description: Understand and calculate the probabilities of Type I and Type II errors in hypothesis tests.

nodes.name: Calculating Type I and Type II Errors
path: Type I and Type II Errors
Calculating Type I and Type II Errors
nodes.description: Calculate the probability of Type I and Type II errors and interpret them in context.

nodes.description: Understand the concept of the power of a test and use it to assess the effectiveness of a hypothesis test.

nodes.name: Calculating the Power of a Test
path: Calculating the Power of a Test
nodes.description: Calculate the power of a test for given distributions and parameters; interpret the power function.

nodes.name: Contingency Tables
nodes.description: Use chi-squared tests for independence in contingency tables.

nodes.description: Perform chi-squared tests for independence in contingency tables and interpret the results.

nodes.description: Calculate and interpret Spearman's rank correlation coefficient for ranked data.

nodes.description: Calculate Spearman's rank correlation coefficient, including handling tied ranks, and interpret its value.

nodes.name: Hypothesis Tests for Correlation
nodes.description: Conduct hypothesis tests for zero correlation using product moment and Spearman correlation coefficients.

nodes.name: Hypothesis Tests for Product Moment Correlation
path: Hypothesis Tests for Product Moment Correlation
nodes.description: Test the null hypothesis that the population product moment correlation coefficient is zero.

nodes.description: Use residuals to assess the fit of a linear model and refine regression models.

nodes.name: Exponential Distribution

nodes.description: Use given substitutions to reduce a differential equation to a solvable form.

nodes.name: Reduction to Linear or Separable Form
path: Reduction to Linear or Separable Form
nodes.description: Apply substitutions to reduce differential equations to linear, separable or constant coefficient form.

nodes.description: Use standard results and the method of differences to sum finite and infinite series.

path: Standard Sums and Method of Differences
nodes.description: Sum series using sums of integers, squares and cubes, and the method of differences including partial fractions.

nodes.description: Solve modulus and rational inequalities algebraically and graphically.

nodes.description: Sketch and identify loci such as |z-a|=k, arg(z-a)=θ and regions defined by inequalities.

nodes.name: Complex Transformations
nodes.description: Apply elementary transformations from the z-plane to the w-plane.

nodes.name: Möbius and Elementary Transformations
nodes.description: Transform curves and regions using w = z², w = (az+b)/(cz+d) and related mappings.

nodes.name: De Moivre's Theorem for Series and Multiple Angles
path: De Moivre's Theorem for Series and Multiple Angles
nodes.description: Use de Moivre's theorem to derive multiple-angle formulae and sum trigonometric series.

nodes.description: Solve three linear simultaneous equations and interpret geometric arrangements of planes.

nodes.description: Use the vector product to find areas of triangles and parallelograms.

nodes.description: Find the shortest distance between skew lines and the equation of the common perpendicular.

nodes.name: Volume of Tetrahedron and Parallelepiped
nodes.description: Use the scalar triple product to find volumes of tetrahedra and parallelepipeds.

nodes.name: Distances and Angles Between Lines and Planes

nodes.description: Find tangents parallel or perpendicular to the initial line for polar curves.

nodes.name: t-Formulae and Further Identities
nodes.description: Use the tangent half-angle substitution and related identities to solve equations and integrals.

nodes.description: Apply L'Hôpital's rule to evaluate limits of indeterminate forms.

nodes.name: Leibniz's Theorem
nodes.description: Differentiate products of functions using Leibniz's theorem for repeated differentiation.

nodes.name: Improper Integrals
nodes.description: Evaluate integrals where the integrand is undefined or the range is infinite.

nodes.name: Arc Length and Surface Area of Revolution
path: Arc Length and Surface Area of Revolution
nodes.description: Derive and calculate arc lengths and surface areas of revolution for Cartesian and parametric curves.

nodes.name: Mean Value of a Function
nodes.description: Evaluate the mean value of a function over an interval.

nodes.name: Taylor Series
nodes.description: Derive and use Taylor series expanded about a general point.

nodes.name: Series and Limits
nodes.description: Use series expansions to evaluate limits and derive inequalities.

nodes.name: Numerical Differential Equations
nodes.description: Approximate solutions of first and second order differential equations using numerical methods.

nodes.name: Euler and Improved Euler Methods
nodes.description: Apply Euler and improved Euler step-by-step methods to first order differential equations.

nodes.description: Use Simpson's rule and finite difference approximations to solve second order differential equations.

nodes.description: Approximate definite integrals using Simpson's rule and estimate the error.

nodes.description: Identify isomorphisms between groups and apply Lagrange's theorem to subgroups.

nodes.name: Euclidean Algorithm and Bezout's Identity
nodes.description: Use the Euclidean algorithm and Bezout's identity for highest common factors and modular inverses.

nodes.name: Fermat's Little Theorem
nodes.description: Apply Fermat's little theorem to congruences and modular arithmetic problems.

nodes.name: Dynamic Programming
nodes.description: Solve multi-stage decision problems using dynamic programming with stage and state variables.

nodes.description: Identify stages, states and actions for a dynamic programming problem.

nodes.name: Maximin and Minimax Objectives
nodes.description: Apply maximin, minimax or minimum/maximum sum objectives in dynamic programming.

nodes.name: Solve Dynamic Programming Problems
nodes.description: Build and interpret dynamic programming tables to find optimal strategies.

nodes.name: Network Flows
nodes.description: Model and solve network flow problems including maximum flow and minimum cut.

nodes.name: Maximum Flow-Minimum Cut
nodes.description: Use flow-augmenting paths and cuts to find the maximum flow through a network.

nodes.name: Supersource and Supersink
nodes.description: Add supersource and supersink vertices to solve flow problems with multiple sources and sinks.

nodes.name: Transportation and Assignment Problems
nodes.description: Solve allocation problems including transportation and assignment using specialist algorithms.

nodes.name: Transportation Problem Algorithm
nodes.description: Find an initial feasible solution and improve it to minimise transportation costs.

nodes.name: Hungarian Algorithm
nodes.description: Use the Hungarian algorithm to solve allocation/assignment problems with a square cost matrix.

nodes.description: Solve linear programming problems in more than two variables using the simplex method.

nodes.name: Simplex Tableau Iterations
nodes.description: Perform simplex iterations using a tableau, choosing pivot rows and columns correctly.

nodes.name: Two-Stage Simplex
nodes.description: Apply the two-stage simplex method to problems with constraints requiring artificial variables.

nodes.name: Fixed-Point Iteration
nodes.description: Rearrange f(x)=0 into x=g(x) form and use fixed-point iteration to find a root.
